text: In this dimly lit corridor, I feel a sense of quiet anticipation tinged with slight unease—like I am on the boundary between the familiar and the unknown. The glass doors and industrial lights give the space a sterile, almost otherworldly atmosphere. As I move into the next room, the transition from darkness to the glowing, colorful wall of the exhibition is both inviting and disorienting. The displays about research, development, and our relationship with intelligent machines invoke curiosity in me, but also a reflection on how technology shapes our present and future. I feel like both an observer and a participant in this evolving dialogue between humans and machines.
